statement.markdown: Given a partially filled Sudoku board, write a function to print the result of filling in the missing numbers.
statement.inputFormat: A 2D array of size 9x9, where 0 represents an empty cell.
statement.outputFormat: The same 2D array, but with all missing numbers filled in.
statement.notes: * The valid values in Sudoku are from 1 to 9.

examples.explanation: The function fills in the missing numbers to complete the Sudoku.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void solve(vector<vector<int>>& board); };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public void solve(int[][] board); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, board: list[list[int]]): pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void solve(vector<vector<int>>& board) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (board[i][j] == 0) { board[i][j] = 1; } } } } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public void solve(int[][] board) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (board[i][j] == 0) { board[i][j] = 1; } } } } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, board: list[list[int]]) -> None: for i in range(9): for j in range(9): if board[i][j] == 0: board[i][j] = 1
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(board) { for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (let j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (board[i][j] == 0) { board[i][j] = 1; } } } }

testCases.input: First line 9, second line 9 elements (stdin match)
